name: AWS Lambda
summary: Production monitoring, observability, and security adoption pack for serverless Lambda workloads.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Observability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Track function health, invocation volume, errors, throttles, duration, and concurrency.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Explainability
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Trace event source, function execution, and downstream service calls with X-Ray or OpenTelemetry.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Quality
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Measure success rate, latency, retry behavior, and dead-letter or destination failures.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Ethics and Safety
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Validate least privilege, secrets handling, environment variables, and auditability.

trusted_ai_pillars.name: Continuous Validation
trusted_ai_pillars.outcome: Continuously validate runtime health, dependency behavior, and deployment safety.

sections.name: Invocation Overview
sections.purpose: Invocation count, success rate, errors, retries, and event-source health.

sections.name: Latency and Runtime
sections.purpose: Duration, p95/p99 latency, timeout proximity, memory use, and cold-start indicators.

sections.name: Concurrency and Throttling
sections.purpose: Concurrent executions, unreserved concurrency pressure, throttles, and reserved concurrency limits.

sections.name: Failures and Destinations
sections.purpose: Dead-letter queue volume, async destination failures, iterator age, and failed batches.

sections.name: Traces and Dependencies
sections.purpose: X-Ray traces, downstream latency, dependency errors, and function segment faults.

sections.name: Security Posture
sections.purpose: IAM role scope, environment variable exposure, CloudTrail changes, and Config/Security Hub findings.

metrics.name: Invocations
metrics.why: Measure workload volume and usage pattern.

metrics.name: Errors
metrics.why: Detect failed executions and application exceptions.

metrics.name: Duration
metrics.why: Track latency, timeout risk, and performance regression.

metrics.name: ConcurrentExecutions
metrics.why: Understand scaling pressure and concurrency saturation.

metrics.name: IteratorAge
metrics.why: Detect stream backlog for Kinesis or DynamoDB event sources.

log_queries.name: Lambda errors and exceptions
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @message | filter @message like /(?i)(error|exception|traceback|task timed out)/ | stats count() by @message | sort count desc | limit 20

log_queries.name: Cold start indicators
log_queries.intent: Find initialization duration from Lambda report logs.
log_queries.query: fields @timestamp, @initDuration, @duration, @maxMemoryUsed | filter ispresent(@initDuration) | sort @initDuration desc | limit 50

alarms.name: LambdaErrorRateHigh
alarms.condition: Errors greater than expected baseline for 5 minutes
alarms.action: Notify owner and inspect logs, traces, and recent deployments.

alarms.name: LambdaDurationNearTimeout
alarms.action: Review slow dependencies, timeout setting, and memory allocation.

alarms.name: LambdaThrottlesDetected
alarms.condition: Throttles greater than zero for 5 minutes
alarms.action: Review reserved concurrency, account concurrency, and event-source rate.

alarms.name: LambdaIteratorAgeHigh
alarms.action: Investigate stream backlog, batch failures, and downstream latency.

security_controls.name: Execution role least privilege
security_controls.evidence: Lambda execution role grants only required actions on scoped resources.

security_controls.name: Secrets management
security_controls.evidence: Secrets are stored in Secrets Manager or Parameter Store instead of plaintext environment variables.

security_controls.name: CloudTrail auditability
security_controls.evidence: Function updates, permission changes, and invocation policy changes are visible in CloudTrail.

security_controls.name: Network control
security_controls.evidence: VPC-attached functions use controlled subnets and security groups when private resources are accessed.

security_controls.name: Dependency hygiene
security_controls.evidence: Deployment package or layer dependencies are scanned in CI before production release.

adoption_plan: Enable structured JSON logging for Lambda functions.
Create CloudWatch alarms for errors, duration, throttles, and backlog where applicable.
Enable X-Ray or OpenTelemetry tracing for critical functions.
Connect alarms to the incident channel or ticketing workflow.
Review execution role scope and secrets handling with the security team.
Create a standard Lambda dashboard template and runbook.
Promote the pack as the default serverless monitoring standard.
